verses.sanskrit: शौनक उवाचअश्वत्थाम्नोपसृष्टेन ब्रह्मशीर्ष्णोरुतेजसा ।उत्तराया हतो गर्भ ईशेनाजीवित: पुन: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaunaka uvācaaśvatthāmnopasṛṣṭenabrahma-śīrṣṇoru-tejasāuttarāyā hato garbhaīśenājīvitaḥ punaḥ
verses.text: The sage Śaunaka said: The womb of Uttarā, mother of Mahārāja Parīkṣit, was spoiled by the dreadful and invincible brahmāstra weapon released by Aśvatthāmā. But Mahārāja Parīkṣit was saved by the Supreme Lord.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य जन्म महाबुद्धे: कर्माणि च महात्मन: ।निधनं च यथैवासीत्स प्रेत्य गतवान् यथा ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya janma mahā-buddheḥkarmāṇi ca mahātmanaḥnidhanaṁ ca yathaivāsītsa pretya gatavān yathā
verses.text: How was the great emperor Parīkṣit, who was a highly intelligent and great devotee, born in that womb? How did his death take place, and what did he achieve after his death?

verses.sanskrit: तदिदं श्रोतुमिच्छामो गदितुं यदि मन्यसे ।ब्रूहि न: श्रद्दधानानां यस्य ज्ञानमदाच्छुक: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad idaṁ śrotum icchāmogadituṁ yadi manyasebrūhi naḥ śraddadhānānāṁyasya jñānam adāc chukaḥ
verses.text: We all respectfully want to hear about him [Mahārāja Parīkṣit] to whom Śukadeva Gosvāmī imparted transcendental knowledge. Please speak on this matter.

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचअपीपलद्धर्मराज: पितृवद् रञ्जयन् प्रजा: ।नि:स्पृह: सर्वकामेभ्य: कृष्णपादानुसेवया ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaapīpalad dharma-rājaḥpitṛvad rañjayan prajāḥniḥspṛhaḥ sarva-kāmebhyaḥkṛṣṇa-pādānusevayā
verses.text: Śrī Sūta Gosvāmī said: Emperor Yudhiṣṭhira administered generously to everyone during his reign. He was exactly like his father. He had no personal ambition and was freed from all sorts of sense gratification because of his continuous service unto the lotus feet of the Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

verses.sanskrit: सम्पद: क्रतवो लोका महिषी भ्रातरो मही ।जम्बूद्वीपाधिपत्यं च यशश्च त्रिदिवं गतम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sampadaḥ kratavo lokāmahiṣī bhrātaro mahījambudvīpādhipatyaṁ cayaśaś ca tri-divaṁ gatam
verses.text: News even reached the celestial planets about Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira’s worldly possessions, the sacrifices by which he would attain a better destination, his queen, his stalwart brothers, his extensive land, his sovereignty over the planet earth, his fame, etc.

verses.sanskrit: किं ते कामा: सुरस्पार्हा मुकुन्दमनसो द्विजा: ।अधिजह्रुर्मुदं राज्ञ: क्षुधितस्य यथेतरे ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ te kāmāḥ sura-spārhāmukunda-manaso dvijāḥadhijahrur mudaṁ rājñaḥkṣudhitasya yathetare
verses.text: O brāhmaṇas, the opulence of the King was so enchanting that the denizens of heaven aspired for it. But because he was absorbed in the service of the Lord, nothing could satisfy him except the Lord’s service.

verses.sanskrit: मातुर्गर्भगतो वीर: स तदा भृगुनन्दन ।ददर्श पुरुषं कञ्चिद्दह्यमानोऽस्त्रतेजसा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: mātur garbha-gato vīraḥsa tadā bhṛgu-nandanadadarśa puruṣaṁ kañciddahyamāno ’stra-tejasā
verses.text: O son of Bhṛgu [Śaunaka], when the child Parīkṣit, the great fighter, was in the womb of his mother, Uttarā, and was suffering from the burning heat of the brahmāstra [thrown by Aśvatthāmā], he could observe the Supreme Lord coming to him.

verses.sanskrit: अङ्गुष्ठमात्रममलं स्फुरत्पुरटमौलिनम् ।अपीव्यदर्शनं श्यामं तडिद्वाससमच्युतम् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: aṅguṣṭha-mātram amalaṁsphurat-puraṭa-maulinamapīvya-darśanaṁ śyāmaṁtaḍid vāsasam acyutam
verses.text: He [the Lord] was only thumb high, but He was all transcendental. He had a very beautiful, blackish, infallible body, and He wore a dress of lightning yellow and a helmet of blazing gold. Thus He was seen by the child.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीमद्दीर्घचतुर्बाहुं तप्तकाञ्चनकुण्डलम् ।क्षतजाक्षं गदापाणिमात्मन: सर्वतोदिशम् ।परिभ्रमन्तमुल्काभां भ्रामयन्तं गदां मुहु: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrīmad-dīrgha-catur-bāhuṁtapta-kāñcana-kuṇḍalamkṣatajākṣaṁ gadā-pāṇimātmanaḥ sarvato diśamparibhramantam ulkābhāṁbhrāmayantaṁ gadāṁ muhuḥ
verses.text: The Lord was enriched with four hands, earrings of molten gold and eyes blood red with fury. As He loitered about, His club constantly encircled Him like a shooting star.

verses.sanskrit: अस्त्रतेज: स्वगदया नीहारमिव गोपति: ।विधमन्तं सन्निकर्षे पर्यैक्षत क इत्यसौ ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: astra-tejaḥ sva-gadayānīhāram iva gopatiḥvidhamantaṁ sannikarṣeparyaikṣata ka ity asau
verses.text: The Lord was thus engaged in vanquishing the radiation of the brahmāstra, just as the sun evaporates a drop of dew. He was observed by the child, who thought about who He was.

verses.sanskrit: विधूय तदमेयात्मा भगवान्धर्मगुब् विभु: ।मिषतो दशमासस्य तत्रैवान्तर्दधे हरि: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidhūya tad ameyātmābhagavān dharma-gub vibhuḥmiṣato daśamāsasyatatraivāntardadhe hariḥ
verses.text: While thus being observed by the child, the Supreme Lord Personality of Godhead, the Supersoul of everyone and the protector of the righteous, who stretches in all directions and who is unlimited by time and space, disappeared at once.

verses.sanskrit: तत: सर्वगुणोदर्के सानुकूलग्रहोदये ।जज्ञे वंशधर: पाण्डोर्भूय: पाण्डुरिवौजसा ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ sarva-guṇodarkesānukūla-grahodayejajñe vaṁśa-dharaḥ pāṇḍorbhūyaḥ pāṇḍur ivaujasā
verses.text: Thereupon, when all the good signs of the zodiac gradually evolved, the heir apparent of Pāṇḍu, who would be exactly like him in prowess, took birth.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य प्रीतमना राजा विप्रैर्धौम्यकृपादिभि: ।जातकं कारयामास वाचयित्वा च मङ्गलम् ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya prīta-manā rājāviprair dhaumya-kṛpādibhiḥjātakaṁ kārayām āsavācayitvā ca maṅgalam
verses.text: King Yudhiṣṭhira, who was very satisfied with the birth of Mahārāja Parīkṣit, had the purificatory process of birth performed. Learned brāhmaṇas, headed by Dhaumya and Kṛpa, recited auspicious hymns.

verses.sanskrit: हिरण्यं गां महीं ग्रामान् हस्त्यश्वान्नृपतिर्वरान् ।प्रादात्स्वन्नं च विप्रेभ्य: प्रजातीर्थे स तीर्थवित् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: hiraṇyaṁ gāṁ mahīṁ grāmānhasty-aśvān nṛpatir varānprādāt svannaṁ ca viprebhyaḥprajā-tīrthe sa tīrthavit
verses.text: Upon the birth of a son, the King, who knew how, where and when charity should be given, gave gold, land, villages, elephants, horses and good food grains to the brāhmaṇas.

verses.sanskrit: तमूचुर्ब्राह्मणास्तुष्टा राजानं प्रश्रयान्वितम् ।एष ह्यस्मिन् प्रजातन्तौ पुरूणां पौरवर्षभ ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam ūcur brāhmaṇās tuṣṭārājānaṁ praśrayānvitameṣa hy asmin prajā-tantaupurūṇāṁ pauravarṣabha
verses.text: The learned brāhmaṇas, who were very satisfied with the charities of the King, addressed him as the chief amongst the Pūrus and informed him that his son was certainly in the line of descent from the Pūrus.

verses.sanskrit: दैवेनाप्रतिघातेन शुक्ले संस्थामुपेयुषि ।रातो वोऽनुग्रहार्थाय विष्णुना प्रभविष्णुना ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: daivenāpratighātenaśukle saṁsthām upeyuṣirāto vo ’nugrahārthāyaviṣṇunā prabhaviṣṇunā
verses.text: The brāhmaṇas said: This spotless son has been restored by the all-powerful and all-pervasive Lord Viṣṇu, the Personality of Godhead, in order to oblige you. He was saved when he was doomed to be destroyed by an irresistible supernatural weapon.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान्नाम्ना विष्णुरात इति लोके भविष्यति ।न सन्देहो महाभाग महाभागवतो महान् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmān nāmnā viṣṇu-rātaiti loke bhaviṣyatina sandeho mahā-bhāgamahā-bhāgavato mahān
verses.text: For this reason this child will be well known in the world as one who is protected by the Personality of Godhead. O most fortunate one, there is no doubt that this child will become a first-class devotee and will be qualified with all good qualities.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचअप्येष वंश्यान् राजर्षीन् पुण्यश्लोकान् महात्मन: ।अनुवर्तिता स्विद्यशसा साधुवादेन सत्तमा: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācaapy eṣa vaṁśyān rājarṣīnpuṇya-ślokān mahātmanaḥanuvartitā svid yaśasāsādhu-vādena sattamāḥ
verses.text: The good King [Yudhiṣṭhira] inquired: O great souls, will he become as saintly a king, as pious in his very name and as famous and glorified in his achievements, as others who appeared in this great royal family?

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणा ऊचु:पार्थ प्रजाविता साक्षादिक्ष्वाकुरिव मानव: ।ब्रह्मण्य: सत्यसन्धश्च रामो दाशरथिर्यथा ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇā ūcuḥpārtha prajāvitā sākṣādikṣvākur iva mānavaḥbrahmaṇyaḥ satya-sandhaś carāmo dāśarathir yathā
verses.text: The learned brāhmaṇas said: O son of Pṛthā, this child shall be exactly like King Ikṣvāku, son of Manu, in maintaining all those who are born. And as for following the brahminical principles, especially in being true to his promise, he shall be exactly like Rāma, the Personality of Godhead, the son of Mahārāja Daśaratha.

verses.sanskrit: एष दाता शरण्यश्च यथा ह्यौशीनर: शिबि: ।यशो वितनिता स्वानां दौष्यन्तिरिव यज्वनाम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣa dātā śaraṇyaś cayathā hy auśīnaraḥ śibiḥyaśo vitanitā svānāṁdauṣyantir iva yajvanām
verses.text: This child will be a munificent donor of charity and protector of the surrendered, like the famous King Śibi of the Uśīnara country. And he will expand the name and fame of his family like Bharata, the son of Mahārāja Duṣyanta.

verses.sanskrit: धन्विनामग्रणीरेष तुल्यश्चार्जुनयोर्द्वयो: ।हुताश इव दुर्धर्ष: समुद्र इव दुस्तर: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhanvinām agraṇīr eṣatulyaś cārjunayor dvayoḥhutāśa iva durdharṣaḥsamudra iva dustaraḥ
verses.text: Amongst great bowmen, this child will be as good as Arjuna. He will be as irresistible as fire and as unsurpassable as the ocean.

verses.sanskrit: मृगेन्द्र इव विक्रान्तो निषेव्यो हिमवानिव ।तितिक्षुर्वसुधेवासौ सहिष्णु: पितराविव ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: mṛgendra iva vikrāntoniṣevyo himavān ivatitikṣur vasudhevāsausahiṣṇuḥ pitarāv iva
verses.text: This child will be as strong as a lion, and as worthy a shelter as the Himālaya Mountains. He will be forbearing like the earth, and as tolerant as his parents.

verses.sanskrit: पितामहसम: साम्ये प्रसादे गिरिशोपम: ।आश्रय: सर्वभूतानां यथा देवो रमाश्रय: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitāmaha-samaḥ sāmyeprasāde giriśopamaḥāśrayaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁyathā devo ramāśrayaḥ
verses.text: This child will be like his grandfather Yudhiṣṭhira or Brahmā in equanimity of mind. He will be munificent like the lord of the Kailāsa Hill, Śiva. And he will be the resort of everyone, like the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Nārāyaṇa, who is even the shelter of the goddess of fortune.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वसद्गुणमाहात्म्ये एष कृष्णमनुव्रत: ।रन्तिदेव इवोदारो ययातिरिव धार्मिक: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarva-sad-guṇa-māhātmyeeṣa kṛṣṇam anuvrataḥrantideva ivodāroyayātir iva dhārmikaḥ
verses.text: This child will be almost as good as Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa by following in His footsteps. In magnanimity he will become as great as King Rantideva. And in religion he will be like Mahārāja Yayāti.

verses.sanskrit: धृत्या बलिसम: कृष्णे प्रह्राद इव सद्ग्रह: ।आहर्तैषोऽश्वमेधानां वृद्धानां पर्युपासक: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhṛtyā bali-samaḥ kṛṣṇeprahrāda iva sad-grahaḥāhartaiṣo ’śvamedhānāṁvṛddhānāṁ paryupāsakaḥ
verses.text: This child will be like Bali Mahārāja in patience, a staunch devotee of Lord Kṛṣṇa like Prahlāda Mahārāja, a performer of many aśvamedha [horse] sacrifices and a follower of the old and experienced men.

verses.sanskrit: राजर्षीणां जनयिता शास्ता चोत्पथगामिनाम् ।निग्रहीता कलेरेष भुवो धर्मस्य कारणात् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājarṣīṇāṁ janayitāśāstā cotpatha-gāmināmnigrahītā kaler eṣabhuvo dharmasya kāraṇāt
verses.text: This child will be the father of kings who will be like sages. For world peace and for the sake of religion, he will be the chastiser of the upstarts and the quarrelsome.

verses.sanskrit: तक्षकादात्मनो मृत्युं द्विजपुत्रोपसर्जितात् ।प्रपत्स्यत उपश्रुत्य मुक्तसङ्ग: पदं हरे: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: takṣakād ātmano mṛtyuṁdvija-putropasarjitātprapatsyata upaśrutyamukta-saṅgaḥ padaṁ hareḥ
verses.text: After hearing about his death, which will be caused by the bite of a snakebird sent by a son of a brāhmaṇa, he will get himself freed from all material attachment and surrender unto the Personality of Godhead, taking shelter of Him.

verses.sanskrit: जिज्ञासितात्मयाथार्थ्यो मुनेर्व्याससुतादसौ ।हित्वेदं नृप गङ्गायां यास्यत्यद्धाकुतोभयम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: jijñāsitātma-yāthārthyomuner vyāsa-sutād asauhitvedaṁ nṛpa gaṅgāyāṁyāsyaty addhākutobhayam
verses.text: After inquiring about proper self-knowledge from the son of Vyāsadeva, who will be a great philosopher, he will renounce all material attachment and achieve a life of fearlessness.

verses.sanskrit: इति राज्ञ उपादिश्य विप्रा जातककोविदा: ।लब्धापचितय: सर्वे प्रतिजग्मु: स्वकान् गृहान् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti rājña upādiśyaviprā jātaka-kovidāḥlabdhāpacitayaḥ sarvepratijagmuḥ svakān gṛhān
verses.text: Thus those who were expert in astrological knowledge and in performance of the birth ceremony instructed King Yudhiṣṭhira about the future history of his child. Then, being sumptuously remunerated, they all returned to their respective homes.

verses.sanskrit: स एष लोके विख्यात: परीक्षिदिति यत्प्रभु: ।पूर्वं द‍ृष्टमनुध्यायन् परीक्षेत नरेष्विह ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: sa eṣa loke vikhyātaḥparīkṣid iti yat prabhuḥpūrvaṁ dṛṣṭam anudhyāyanparīkṣeta nareṣv iha
verses.text: So his son would become famous in the world as Parīkṣit [examiner] because he would come to examine all human beings in his search after that personality whom he saw before his birth. Thus he would come to constantly contemplate Him.

verses.sanskrit: स राजपुत्रो ववृधे आशु शुक्ल इवोडुप: ।आपूर्यमाण: पितृभि: काष्ठाभिरिव सोऽन्वहम् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa rāja-putro vavṛdheāśu śukla ivoḍupaḥāpūryamāṇaḥ pitṛbhiḥkāṣṭhābhir iva so ’nvaham
verses.text: As the moon, in its waxing fortnight, develops day after day, so the royal prince [Parīkṣit] very soon developed luxuriantly under the care and full facilities of his guardian grandfathers.

verses.sanskrit: यक्ष्यमाणोऽश्वमेधेन ज्ञातिद्रोहजिहासया ।राजा लब्धधनो दध्यौ नान्यत्र करदण्डयो: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yakṣyamāṇo ’śvamedhenajñāti-droha-jihāsayārājā labdha-dhano dadhyaunānyatra kara-daṇḍayoḥ
verses.text: Just at this time, King Yudhiṣṭhira was considering performing a horse sacrifice to get freed from sins incurred from fighting with kinsmen. But he became anxious to get some wealth, for there were no surplus funds outside of fines and tax collection.

verses.sanskrit: तदभिप्रेतमालक्ष्य भ्रातरोऽच्युतचोदिता: ।धनं प्रहीणमाजह्रुरुदीच्यां दिशि भूरिश: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad abhipretam ālakṣyabhrātaro ’cyuta-coditāḥdhanaṁ prahīṇam ājahrurudīcyāṁ diśi bhūriśaḥ
verses.text: Understanding the hearty wishes of the King, his brothers, as advised by the infallible Lord Kṛṣṇa, collected sufficient riches from the north [left by King Marutta].

verses.sanskrit: तेन सम्भृतसम्भारो धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिर: ।वाजिमेधैस्त्रिभिर्भीतो यज्ञै: समयजद्धरिम् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tena sambhṛta-sambhārodharma-putro yudhiṣṭhiraḥvājimedhais tribhir bhītoyajñaiḥ samayajad dharim
verses.text: By those riches, the King could procure the ingredients for three horse sacrifices. Thus the pious King Yudhiṣṭhira, who was very fearful after the Battle of Kurukṣetra, pleased Lord Hari, the Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: आहूतो भगवान् राज्ञा याजयित्वा द्विजैर्नृपम् ।उवास कतिचिन्मासान् सुहृदां प्रियकाम्यया ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: āhūto bhagavān rājñāyājayitvā dvijair nṛpamuvāsa katicin māsānsuhṛdāṁ priya-kāmyayā
verses.text: Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead, being invited to the sacrifices by Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, saw to it that they were performed by qualified [twice-born] brāhmaṇas. After that, for the pleasure of the relatives, the Lord remained a few months.

verses.sanskrit: ततो राज्ञाभ्यनुज्ञात: कृष्णया सह बन्धुभि: ।ययौ द्वारवतीं ब्रह्मन् सार्जुनो यदुभिर्वृत: ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato rājñābhyanujñātaḥkṛṣṇayā saha-bandhubhiḥyayau dvāravatīṁ brahmansārjuno yadubhir vṛtaḥ
verses.text: O Śaunaka, thereafter the Lord, having bade farewell to King Yudhiṣṭhira, Draupadī and other relatives, started for the city of Dvārakā, accompanied by Arjuna and other members of the Yadu dynasty.
